specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI Chat Completions request builder serializes a canonical LLM request into the Chat Completions API JSON format, applying schema transformations required for strict mode compatibility.

scope.in-scope: The structural layout of the Chat Completions request body, the tool schema transformations applied for strict mode, tool choice mapping, message assembly order, streaming configuration, and URL and header construction.
scope.boundary: How individual canonical messages are transformed into the Chat Completions message object format is covered by the OpenAI message serialization specification.
The decision of whether to use the Chat Completions endpoint versus the Responses endpoint is covered by the OpenAI API routing specification.
The Chat Completions API does not carry a reasoning configuration object. Reasoning effort for Chat Completions models is not part of this serializer.

field.name: name
field.note: function name as sent to the API

field.name: parameters
field.note: JSON Schema string describing the function's parameters

field.name: messages
field.note: ordered list of message objects; begins with system message if present

field.name: max_completion_tokens
field.note: present only when a positive token limit is set

field.name: stream
field.note: present and true only for streaming requests

field.name: stream_options
field.note: present only for streaming requests; contains include_usage: true

field.name: tools
field.note: present only when one or more tools are defined

field.name: tool_choice
field.note: present only when tools are defined

field.name: parallel_tool_calls
field.note: present only when tools are defined; always true

behavior.schema-transformation:
Before any tool definition is included in the request, its parameter schema is transformed to satisfy the requirements of OpenAI strict mode. The transformations are applied in order:
First, all "format" fields are removed from the schema recursively. Format validators such as "uri" are valid JSON Schema but are rejected by the OpenAI API in strict mode. The removal traverses the full schema graph: the top-level schema object, every property value within a "properties" object, the "items" object for array types, and every element of "oneOf", "anyOf", and "allOf" arrays. Nesting is handled recursively at each level encountered during traversal.
Second, the "required" array is replaced wholesale with a new array that contains the name of every key present in the "properties" object. Any previously existing "required" array is discarded. If the schema has no "properties" object the required array is left unchanged.
Third, "additionalProperties": false is appended to the parameter schema object.
The existing "required" array, if any, is discarded and rebuilt from scratch using the current set of property keys. Properties that were not previously required become required. No attempt is made to merge the old and new lists.
The "format" key is removed from every object node visited in the recursive traversal, not only from leaf nodes or top-level property definitions. A schema with nested combinators receives format removal at every depth.
behavior.tool-serialization:
Each tool definition is serialized as an object with two fields: "type" set to the string "function", and "function" containing a nested object. The nested function object contains the tool name, the tool description, the transformed parameter schema under the key "parameters", and "strict": true.
The "strict": true field is placed on the function object alongside "name", "description", and "parameters". It is not placed inside the parameter schema itself.
behavior.tool-choice-mapping:
The canonical tool choice mode is mapped to a string for the "tool_choice" field:
- Mode 0 (auto): "auto" - Mode 1 (none): "none" - Mode 2 (required): "required" - Any unrecognized mode: "auto"
The "tool_choice" field is only included in the request when at least one tool is present. When no tools are defined, "tool_choice" is omitted entirely.
behavior.message-assembly:
The messages array is assembled as follows:
If the system prompt is non-null and non-empty, a message object with role "system" and content set to the system prompt string is prepended as the first element of the messages array.
The canonical conversation messages follow in their original order. Each is serialized according to the message serialization rules for the Chat Completions format.
Unlike the Responses API, the Chat Completions API embeds the system prompt as the first element of the messages array with role "system". There is no separate top-level field for it.
behavior.streaming-configuration:
For streaming requests, two additional fields are added to the request body: "stream": true, and a "stream_options" object containing "include_usage": true. The "include_usage" flag causes the API to include token usage counts in the final streaming chunk, enabling accurate usage reporting at stream completion.
For non-streaming requests, neither "stream" nor "stream_options" is included.
behavior.parallel-tool-calls: When at least one tool is defined, "parallel_tool_calls": true is included in the request body. This field is always set to true and is never conditionally disabled. It is omitted entirely when no tools are present.
behavior.max-tokens-field: When a positive maximum output token count is set on the canonical request, it is written to the field "max_completion_tokens". This field name is specific to the Chat Completions API. When no limit is set the field is omitted.
behavior.url-construction: The endpoint URL is formed by appending "/v1/chat/completions" to the configured base URL.
behavior.header-construction: Two HTTP headers are produced: "Content-Type: application/json" and "Authorization: Bearer {api_key}" where {api_key} is the configured credential. The headers are returned as a null-terminated array of strings.
